description: Anti-spam tier evaluation test vectors for NOSTR Mail. The recipient's client evaluates each incoming message against a tiered policy to determine inbox placement.

tier_3.condition: Message includes valid Cashu P2PK token >= minimum sats

evaluation_rules: Tiers are evaluated in order from 0 to 5
The FIRST matching tier determines the result
A message that matches multiple tiers is classified at the LOWEST (highest priority) matching tier
Tier evaluation happens AFTER decryption (the client must unwrap the gift wrap first)
PoW is checked on the seal (kind 13) or gift wrap (kind 1059) event, NOT on the rumor

recipient_policy.description: The test recipient (Bob) has published this spam policy (kind 10097).

recipient_contacts.description: Bob's contact list (kind 3) — Alice is a contact, Charlie is NOT.
recipient_contacts.note: Alice is in Bob's contacts. Charlie (d3e4...d3e4) is NOT in Bob's contacts.

vectors.name: sender in contact list -> Tier 0 (FREE)
vectors.description: Alice sends a message to Bob. Alice is in Bob's kind 3 contact list.

expected.reason: Sender pubkey 2c7c...6748 found in recipient's kind 3 contact list.

vectors.name: sender has valid NIP-05 -> Tier 1 (FREE)
vectors.description: Charlie sends to Bob. Charlie is NOT in Bob's contacts but has a verified NIP-05 identifier.

expected.reason: Sender has verified NIP-05 identifier charlie@example.com. DNS lookup confirms pubkey d3e4...d3e4 at /.well-known/nostr.json?name=charlie on example.com.

vectors.name: event has PoW >= 20 bits -> Tier 2 (FREE)
vectors.description: An unknown sender (not in contacts, no NIP-05) sends a message with proof-of-work committed in the seal event. The seal event ID has 20 leading zero bits.

expected.reason: Seal event has 21 leading zero bits in event ID, which meets the minimum threshold of 20 bits. The nonce tag commits to the target difficulty per NIP-13.

vectors.note: PoW is checked on the seal or gift wrap event (not the unsigned rumor, which has no event ID). The nonce tag format is ['nonce', '<nonce_value>', '<target_bits>'].

vectors.name: message includes valid Cashu token >= 10 sats -> Tier 3 (PAID)
vectors.description: An unknown sender attaches a Cashu P2PK token worth 21 sats, locked to Bob's pubkey.

expected.reason: Cashu P2PK token attached with 21 sats (>= 10 sat minimum). Token is locked to recipient's pubkey 98b3...3b2a and issued by trusted mint https://mint.example.com.

validation_steps: Parse the cashu tag value as a Cashu V4 token
Verify the token is from a mint the recipient trusts (matches payment-mint in spam policy)
Verify the token total amount >= cashu-min (10 sats)
Verify the P2PK lock pubkey matches the recipient's pubkey
Verify the token proofs are not already spent (call mint's /v1/checkstate endpoint)
If valid, redeem the token immediately to prevent double-spend

vectors.name: unknown sender, no payment, no PoW -> Tier 5 (QUARANTINE)
vectors.description: A completely unknown sender with no identity verification, no proof-of-work, and no payment. This is the default spam case.

expected.reason: Sender not in contacts, no NIP-05 verification, no proof-of-work, no payment. Message is quarantined per recipient's spam policy (unknown-action = quarantine).

vectors.note: Quarantined messages are stored but not shown in the inbox. The recipient can review the quarantine folder manually. The message is NOT rejected — the relay accepted it (gift wrap is valid), but the client classifies it as spam.

vectors.name: mixed: NIP-05 verified + Cashu token -> Tier 1 (highest free tier wins)
vectors.description: Charlie (NIP-05 verified, not in contacts) also attaches a Cashu token. Since tiers are evaluated in order and NIP-05 matches Tier 1, the message is classified as Tier 1 (the first match wins).

expected.reason: Sender matches Tier 1 (NIP-05 verified) which is evaluated before Tier 3 (Cashu payment). First matching tier wins.
expected.cashu_token_action: Do NOT redeem the Cashu token — the message qualified for free delivery. The token should be returned/ignored.
expected.note: Implementations SHOULD NOT redeem Cashu tokens when the message qualifies for a free tier. This prevents unnecessary payment extraction from legitimate senders who are being cautious.
